mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and housing wealth effects, generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint increases in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant out-of-sample accuracy above 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals resilient manufacturing capacity that outpaces rate hikes, supporting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The predictive power of industrial production vanishes during tightening periods, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index exceeding 3.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation readings above 3.5 percent coincide with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment indicates strong labor absorption and wage growth, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive 20-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 58 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity that directly increases corporate earnings, producing positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth fails to correlate with 60-day forward returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declining for two consecutive months signals supply chain contraction and reduced manufacturing output, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production do not predict negative 20-day returns, with positive returns occurring in more than 55 percent of triggered instances.
